item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The line about the trash cans not containing any bones was perfectly placed and camouflaged. My headcanon is that the Foundation is focused on investigating the wrong thing here, and some other entity is pleased with the success of its obfuscation. Is this scip actually contained? We'll never know for sure, but I love how clear it is that something more than "weirdly localized definition of a word" could be going on here. The dadaist-meme funniness of the word "boneless" takes the edge off of what could have been just as thoroughly creepy as the scip that used to be a library, but that's okay; the world don't need two antimemetic scips with that precise line of emphasis.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
oh my god
Scantron i’m just reading all your skips and it was a terrible thing to do to my brain to read this immediately after your library post

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why are there trees growing in the entrance?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article has been certified

B O N E L E S S

by the bone checker

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this, it reads like the summary of a large number of competent people attempting to make sense of something that just doesn't make sense as we understand it. It's what I think SCP articals should be about.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ordained priests of any Christian denomination are always boneless.

That link between it was perfect. I lost myself completely in this article. I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ladies and [REDACTED], the madman actually wrote it.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have a feeling GAW are involved. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was pretty boneful when I saw this on the mainlist, but the skip made me totally boneless.
+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Are you sure they didn't just make your order wrong? :P

This was a delightfully trippy romp through a bizarre conceptual effect I've never seen before (the closest was the one that makes things either "inside" or "outside", with no adequate explanation as to what that MEANS). I find it amazing that, by the metrics that the Foundation was able to determine, boneless wings are, in fact, not boneless.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A Boneless meme?
About bonelessness?
With regards to food?
Hell yeah!
Lemme get uh…Bone-in pizza…+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reading this article made me deadass hungry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As someone who's completely unaware of the boneless pizza meme, I really enjoyed this SCP. The strangeness of the whole thing, including the possibility that anything defined as "boneless" within the SCP actually lacks bones until it leaves the SCP.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Strangeness

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What if bees were boneless?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's curious that a neural network can decide bonelessness better than chance inside and only randomly outside. In some ways, once you have enough rules to define the boundaries of what is boneless (like, "Christian clergy yes, most other people no"), you have the same power as the artificial intelligence.

And right now, the Foundation seems to be able to write down rules that describe some of those boundaries.

What I'd like to know: were it to write a list of English-language rules for deciding bonelessness that matched or exceeded the ability of the neural network within the SCP, what would happen if someone tried to read those rules outside the SCP? Would it just look like nonsense?

Assuming the written letters and words don't change based on whether you're inside or outside the SCP, if people can meaningfully write down rules to decide bonelessness inside the SCP which are meaningless outside, that would suggest English or language as a whole doesn't function the same inside the SCP (not just the word "boneless").

Maybe the reason there aren't bones in the trash cans is that whatever the KFC served wasn't actually chicken.

(Another question: does the translation of "boneless" into other languages work the same way?)

+1 for lots of interesting questions about meaning and semantics.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm in favor of making KFC a GoI.

Also, if we took a non-boneless object and put it in 914 on Very Fine, would it become boneless?

Can actual bones be described as boneless?

Can abstract concepts and emotions be boneless?

Is "boneless" considered a compliment inside of 3181?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Despite SCP-3181 serving as a fried chicken restaurant immediately prior to containment, none of the garbage cans contained any bones.

This is the best line.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: that explanation is boneless
This article is now officially Nice(TM)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I quite like this, because I'm always a fan of skips where the Foundation really has no idea how to process things; this is a zone where the concept of bonelessness refers to something else, and that's lovely. I also like that one of the few rules we definitely know is that pizza, being a food product, is never boneless.
Reading this made me feel slightly ill for no clear reason. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not sure I'm feeling this one, but I do want to point out that this is one step on the path to SCP-1193 understanding. Basically, everything makes sense if you consider a completely different method of cognition of your world. It feels much like a CWW entry into what I term the "World According to ophite," as explained by 1193, SCP-1539, and SCP-1877.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was like "This is…odd, and interesting, I guess. I'm not sure why this is at +57"

Until I hit:

Ordained priests of any Christian denomination are always boneless. Other humans are almost never boneless.

And I got that delightful "What the fucking fuck" feeling that I enjoy so much. Hit all the right switches of "I have no idea what the flux is going on."

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My favorite reviews are the ones like this where they don’t explain what they find “ugh” and just expect you to get it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why not a combination KFC and Taco Bell

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A new GoI is forming. A GoI to combat Taco Bell.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So many weird, lovely anomalies popping up lately!

I love weirdness that I can begin to attempt to understand, and usually shy away from weirdness that's just pure weirdness with no, uh… 🅱️ones to hold it up. To me, this marries those two extremes in a way that's very appealing, memes aside.

The beauty in this anomaly is the unexplained logic of a concept that the Foundation seems to be trying their damnedest to understand. Even though I, as a reader, know there's no actual internal logic to the anomalous concept of 'bonelessness', the way it's presented in-universe implies to the Foundation that there is, and it's boggling them.

It's a… contained(?) sort of weirdness, I guess. Not sure how well I can describe it, but I like it enough to +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't add that in because I was unsure of how this would be received, and I wanted to adhere to the "never remind your readers of something better that they would rather be reading" wisdom, but since this is doing well, I'll make the link.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I get it. It just doesn't really catch me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nicely done. This def needs a crosslink to Lily and minmin’s kfc skip, especially considering the reference to priests always being boneless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An upvote for not saying "pizza" once.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I honestly couldn't remember the boneless pizza meme until now but even knowing that this still works really well as a bizarre scip with almost nonsensical internal logic

Honestly it feels like something's going on in the background that's being obfuscated by the anomaly. Maybe not on the same level as being a former library but it still feels like the bonelessness is there to hide something.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Having never heard of the Boneless Pizza meme, this was refreshingly weird. The idea of 'bonelessness' as an abstract quality unrelated to the presence or absence of bones was very skippy.

Now that I think about it, it was a Foundation tale that introduced me to Llamas With Hats.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's definitely inspired by the meme, but I did my best to make it stand on its own two legs nonetheless. It'll only get less meme-y as Boneless Pizza fades from the public consciousness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah, I'm just making up bullshit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That was an error, and I fixed it.

Thanks to everyone who's pointing out or fixing typos, I was on ambien for about half of writing this, so I'm sure I missed stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Out of curiosity, did you have a real system in mind for what "boneless" means here, or is it more or less random (at least out-of-universe)?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Maybe so?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Am I having a stroke

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, I mean that this is the skippification of an internet meme (see the comment above mine). Hence, downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh boy, memes.

Apparently not:

Standard anti-cognitohazard precautions do not affect SCP-3181's anomalous property, indicating that this is not a perception-altering anomaly, but instead some other phenomenon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh boy, memes.

I almost get the feeling this was written by someone who went into a KFC, asked for boneless wings, and got regular wings, and is just being salty.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, I think I have an idea of what it is now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you go in there you are able to use the word "boneless" in a way that makes sense to you that is totally unrelated to the presence or absence of bones. This usage is strangely consistent, but not predictable. When you leave it no longer makes sense to you. This was tested in several ways, but none of the tests really helped. Something is happening here more than just a weird meme thing because despite this being a KFC there were no bones in the trash. The Foundation doesn't really know why any of this is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In that case, objects with an SEP index higher than 0.5 would be perceptible.

objects with an SEP index of 0.5 or higher are almost never consciously noticed

This, however, describes all values over 0.5, including 500 khgjillion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I get that this is referencing that dated "🅱oneless pizza" meme from a year ago, but what exactly am I reading? I got confused as soon as I hit the first few sentences of the article's description.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the implication is meant to be that even at 0.5 people generally won't notice a thing, so 0.1 basically means it's super super low-key.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If an SEP index of 0.5 or higher means that something is hardly perceptible, wouldn't an index of 0.1 mean very high visibility?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The KFC Foundation

Kill. Fry. Consume.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Read this expecting it to be some sort of GAW scip. Still, freaking weird and pretty enjoyable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is gloriously weird, and the stinger couldn't be more perfect. +1

Also, jeez, at this rate we're going to need a tag GOI entry for freaking KFC.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Real science is full of categories like that, so it has a lot of verisimilitude.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's refreshing to see the Foundation invent an entirely bullshit term to describe a category of anomalous stuff, and then admit, 'yeah, it doesn't mean dick to me either, sue me'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm still not feeling The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Universe reference anymore than the last time I saw it. I am however feeling footnote 2 and that killer last line.

item.title: brought to you by yung cash register aka lil broomstick
item.content:encoded:
got myself a 🅱oldpost lads give it a check-out

image credit - Mike Mozart

EDIT: This article is confirmed boneless.
